headlines.title: China’s Crude Imports Set to Hit Weakest Level Since 2016
headlines.summary: Chinese crude oil imports this month are on course to book an even weaker month than May, according to Kpler data, which sees the daily average at just 6.4 million barrels, as cited by Bloomberg. Data from Vortexa suggests a similar daily import level, the publication added. This

headlines.title: Qatar Offers First Crude Loadings to Buyers Since War Began
headlines.summary: Qatar is tendering crude for loading outside the Strait of Hormuz in July and August, in what is likely the first Qatari crude offering to buyers since the Iran war began on February 28, trade sources told Reuters on Friday. headlines.title: Qatar's LNG Recovery Picks Up Speed as Tankers Mass at Ras Laffan
headlines.summary: As many as eight empty LNG carriers have arrived at the Ras Laffan hub in Qatar and are set to load on superchilled gas in the coming days, Bloomberg has reported. headlines.title: Oil Heads for Weekly Loss as Hormuz Tanker Traffic Rebounds
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices were on course for a sharp weekly loss amid multiple reports about a strong rebound in tanker traffic in the Strait of Hormuz. headlines.title: Colombia's Cocaine Trade Now Outearns Its Oil Exports
headlines.summary: Drug lords in Colombia are making more money from cocaine than the government is making from crude oil sales, a report from a Colombian university has found. At $16.5 billion for 2024, cocaine revenues surpassed oil export revenues, which stood at around $15 billion that year, UP

headlines.title: Asia's Crude Buying Spree Is Running Out of Steam
headlines.summary: Asian refiners have reduced their spot purchases of Middle East crude for loading this month and next, following three weeks in which they had purchased millions of barrels of UAE, Saudi, and Iraqi crude. Lingering uncertainties about the navigability of the Strait of Hormuz and

headlines.title: SpaceX Wants to Fuel Its Mars Ambitions With Its Own Gas Pipeline
headlines.summary: SpaceX has spent years trying to reinvent rockets. Now it's coming after midstream. The aerospace giant plans to begin construction next month on an eight-mile natural gas pipeline dubbed "Starpipe" that will feed its Starbase launch facility in South Texas, according to filings

headlines.title: Kazakhstan Lands $12 Billion in EU Deals to Expand Energy and Trade Ties
headlines.summary: Kazakh President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev’s visit to the EU’s headquarters in Brussels has yielded agreements and MoUs potentially valued at over 12 billion dollars.
